html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 高山崩，石自破 - Metaphorical expression symbolizing a significant event or change, possibly political upheaval.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明帝 - Emperor Yuan of Jin (318-325 AD)
  • 东晋 - Eastern Jin Dynasty (317-420 AD)
  • 荀氏 - Author, likely a member of the Xun family
  • 灵鬼志 - Title of the text, a collection of supernatural stories and legends
  • 诛峻 - Historical event where Wang Dun was defeated and killed in 324 AD
  • 石头 - Likely referring to Jiankang (modern-day Nanjing)
  • The text quotes a popular saying or prophecy, common in ancient China for predicting future events.

Proper Nouns

  • 峻 - Likely a proper name, possibly referring to Wang Dun
  • 硕 - Likely a proper name, possibly related to '峻'
  • 石头 - Likely referring to Jiankang (modern-day Nanjing)

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • '高山崩，石自破' is a metaphorical expression that needs to be interpreted in the context of the historical event. It could symbolize the collapse of a powerful figure or regime.
  • The subject of the action '诛峻' is implied but not explicitly stated. It is understood that the '诸公' (various officials) were responsible for the action.
  • '溃散而逃' describes a chaotic and panicked retreat, but the exact circumstances leading to this event are not detailed in the text.
  • '追斩之' has the subject of the action as the '追' (chase), but the identity of the pursuers is not explicitly stated.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 侧侧力 - Colloquial expression conveying effort or struggle, possibly with a tone of desperation or urgency.
  • 放马出山侧 - Metaphor for releasing control or allowing something to escape.
  • 大马死，小马饿 - Symbolic meaning indicating the collapse of a system or hierarchy.
  • 翩翩如飞鸟 - Poetic expression describing someone moving gracefully, like a bird in flight.
  • 白马牵旒旐 - Metaphorical phrase symbolizing a dignified and solemn procession, often associated with mourning or departure.
  • 黄昙英 - Term that may refer to a specific type of flower or plant, but could also be a metaphor for something delicate or fleeting.
  • 扬州大佛来上明 - Poetic line likely referring to a significant figure (the '大佛') arriving at a place ('上明'), possibly symbolizing the arrival of power or influence.
  • 头细面狭腹中宽 - Descriptive phrase painting a vivid picture of a person's appearance, emphasizing their small head, narrow face, and broad chest.
  • 丈八大矛左右盘 - Phrase describing a weapon (a long spear) and its movement, possibly symbolizing strength and agility.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明帝末 - Refers to the end of Emperor Ming's reign, indicating a specific historical period.
  • 石头 - Place name, likely referring to a specific location in China, possibly a strategic or significant site.
  • 庾文康 - Historical figure, likely a prominent official or military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 武昌 - Place name, likely referring to a city in China, possibly a seat of power or a significant location.
  • 庾公 - Respectful title for a prominent individual, likely referring to庾文康.
  • 扬州 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China, possibly a center of power or culture.
  • 河间王颙 - Historical figure, likely a powerful noble or military leader during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 关中 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China, possibly a center of conflict or power.
  • 桓石民 - Historical figure, likely a military leader or official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 荆州 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China, possibly a seat of power or a significant location.
  • 上明 - Place name, likely referring to a specific location in China, possibly a strategic or significant site.
  • 王忱 - Historical figure, likely a prominent official or military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 佛大 - Nickname or title, likely referring to a specific individual, possibly a powerful or influential figure.
  • 河间王颙既败于关中 - Refers to a historical event where 河间王颙 suffered a defeat in 关中, indicating a significant conflict or battle.
  • 陈安 - Historical figure, likely a military leader or official during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 赤马 - Term referring to a red horse, possibly symbolizing strength or speed.
  • 双持二刀 - Descriptive phrase indicating that the individual carried two swords, possibly symbolizing martial prowess.
  • 所向披靡 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'invincible' or 'undefeated,' often used to describe a powerful or unstoppable force.
  • 关西 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China, possibly a center of conflict or power.
  • 垄上健儿字陈安 - Poetic line likely referring to 陈安 as a strong and capable warrior.
  • 头细面狭腹中宽 - Descriptive phrase painting a vivid picture of a person's appearance, emphasizing their small head, narrow face, and broad chest.
  • 丈八大矛左右盘 - Phrase describing a weapon (a long spear) and its movement, possibly symbolizing strength and agility.

Proper Nouns

  • 明帝 - Reign title of Emperor Ming of Eastern Jin.
  • 石头 - Place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 庾文康 - Historical figure, likely a prominent official or military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 武昌 - Place name, likely referring to a city in China.
  • 庾公 - Respectful title for a prominent individual, likely referring to庾文康.
  • 扬州 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China.
  • 河间王颙 - Historical figure, likely a powerful noble or military leader during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 关中 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China.
  • 桓石民 - Historical figure, likely a military leader or official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 荆州 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China.
  • 上明 - Place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 王忱 - Historical figure, likely a prominent official or military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 佛大 - Nickname or title, likely referring to a specific individual.
  • 陈安 - Historical figure, likely a military leader or official during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 赤马 - Term referring to a red horse.
  • 关西 - Place name, likely referring to a region in China.
  • 垄上健儿 - Poetic term referring to a strong and capable warrior.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甚壯健 - Very strong and healthy; emphasizes physical prowess.
  • 常樂一馬，骏駃非常 - Often enjoyed a horse, which was exceptionally swift and powerful; '骏駃' describes a horse that is both fast and strong.
  • 吾耻与魑魅争光 - I am ashamed to compete in brightness with demons and ghosts; '魑魅' refers to evil spirits or demons.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈安 - A historical figure, but specific details about him are not provided in the text.
  • 河间王颙 - Prince Yen of Hejian, a historical figure during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 嵇康 - A famous philosopher, poet, and musician of the Wei and Jin periods; known for his opposition to the ruling class and his tragic death.
  • 魑魅 - Evil spirits or demons, often used in classical literature to describe supernatural beings.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈安 - Tchen An
  • 河间王颙 - Prince Yen of Hejian
  • 嵇康 - Ji Kang

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中散 - A title or nickname, likely referring to a scholar or musician.
  • 擊節 - A literary device meaning to clap in rhythm or to express admiration, often used in the context of music or poetry.
  • 形骸之間 - A phrase referring to the physical body or material existence.
  • 恍若蹔生 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'as if temporarily reborn' or 'as if momentarily revived.'
  • 心開神悟 - A phrase describing a state of enlightenment or sudden insight.
  • 聲調絕倫 - A phrase meaning 'exceedingly unique in tone' or 'unparalleled in sound.'
  • 半夕悉得 - A phrase meaning 'learned completely in half a night,' emphasizing rapid mastery.
  • 長絕 - A phrase meaning 'permanent separation' or 'eternal parting.'
  • 怅然 - A term describing a feeling of melancholy or regret.
  • 廣陵散 - A famous ancient Chinese musical piece, often associated with the legend of Ruan Ji.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中散 - Likely refers to a scholar or musician, possibly a historical figure or a literary archetype. The exact identity is unclear.
  • 廣陵散 - A legendary musical piece, often associated with the story of Ruan Ji, who is said to have played it before his death. The piece is considered a symbol of artistic mastery and emotional depth.
  • 御覽 - Refers to the *Taiping Yulan* (太平御览), a massive encyclopedic work compiled during the Song Dynasty, which often cites earlier texts.
  • 事类赋注 - Refers to the *Shilie Fu Zhu* (事类赋注), a commentary on the *Shilie Fu* (事类赋), a literary work from the Han Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 中散 - A title or nickname, likely referring to a scholar or musician.
  • 廣陵散 - A famous ancient Chinese musical piece.
  • 御覽 - Refers to the *Taiping Yulan*.
  • 事类赋注 - Refers to the *Shilie Fu Zhu*.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 咸康三年 - Refers to the third year of the Xiankang era during the Eastern Jin Dynasty (343 AD).
  • 子长至寒溪浦中嵇家 - Means 'Zichang went to the house of Jī in the middle of the Cold Creek.'
  • 未达减一里许 - Means 'not quite reaching, about one li less.'
  • 门卒便捉子长头 - Means 'the gatekeeper grabbed Zichang's head.'
  • 我是佛弟子，何故捉我 - Means 'I am a disciple of the Buddha, why are you grabbing me?'
  • 若是佛弟子，能经呗不 - Means 'If you are a disciple of the Buddha, can you chant?'
  • 子长先能诵四天王及鹿子经 - Means 'Zichang could recite the Four Heavenly Kings and the Deer Prince Sutra.'
  • 便为诵之三四过 - Means 'he recited them three or four times.'
  • 捉故不置 - Means 'the gatekeeper still did not let go.'
  • 知是鬼，便骂之曰 - Means 'knowing it was a ghost, he cursed it, saying...'
  • 武昌痴鬼语汝 - Means 'You foolish ghost of Wuchang, tell you...'
  • 我是佛弟子，为汝诵经数偈，故不放人也 - Means 'I am a disciple of the Buddha, I chanted several verses of the sutra for you, so I am not letting you go.'
  • 捉者便放，不复见屋 - Means 'the gatekeeper let go, and the house was no longer seen.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸康三年 - A specific year in the Eastern Jin Dynasty, indicating the historical context.
  • 子长 - A person's name, likely a historical or fictional figure.
  • 寒溪浦中嵇家 - 'Cold Creek' and 'Jī's house' are specific locations, possibly named after a person or a place with cultural significance.
  • 五丈 - A place name, possibly a village or town.
  • 佛弟子 - Refers to a follower of Buddhism.
  • 四天王 - The Four Heavenly Kings, important deities in Buddhism.
  • 鹿子经 - The Deer Prince Sutra, a Buddhist text.
  • 武昌 - A place name, possibly a city or region.

Proper Nouns

  • 咸康三年 - A specific year in the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 子长 - A person's name.
  • 寒溪浦中嵇家 - A specific location.
  • 五丈 - A place name.
  • 四天王 - The Four Heavenly Kings.
  • 鹿子经 - The Deer Prince Sutra.
  • 武昌 - A place name.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鬼故逐之 - Ambiguous phrase: 'because of the ghost' or 'the ghost's reason'; 'chased him/her'.
  • 過家门前 - 'Passed by in front of the house'.
  • 鬼遮不得入 - 'Unable to block or obstruct entry'.
  • 亦不得作声 - 'Unable to make a sound'.
  • 心将鬼至寒溪寺中过 - Ambiguous phrase: 'the ghost's heart' or 'the ghost's intention'; 'passed by at the Cold Stream Temple'.
  • 子长便擒鬼胸 - 'Cursed and said', 'grabbed or seized the ghost's chest'.
  • 复骂曰 - 'Again or once more, cursed and said'.
  • 武昌痴鬼 - 'Foolish ghost of Wuchang'.
  • 今当汝至寺中和尚前了之 - 'Now, should you go before the monk in the temple, settle it'.
  • 捉者已放 - 'The captor has already released'.
  • 广记引作捉者曰已擒不放 - 'Quoted as the captor said, already seized and not released'.
  • 子长故复语后者曰 - 'So or therefore, told the latter again, said'.
  • 寺中正有道人辈 - 'In the temple, just have monks or ascetics'.
  • 乃未肯畏之 - 'Then or thus, not willing to fear it'.
  • 后一鬼小语曰 - 'A later ghost whispered, said'.
  • 汝近城东看道人面 - 'You near the east of the city, see the face of the monk'.
  • 何以败 - 'Why failed or lost'.
  • 便共大笑 - 'Then together, laughed loudly'.
  • 子长比达家 - 'By the time, reached home'.
  • 已三更尽矣 - 'Already the third watch ended'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武昌 - Historical city in present-day Hubei Province, known for its cultural and political significance.
  • 寒溪寺 - The name of a temple, likely a specific location in the narrative.
  • 法苑珠林 - A Buddhist encyclopedia compiled by Xuanzang, containing a wide range of Buddhist teachings and stories.
  • 广记 - Likely refers to the '太平广记' (Taiping Guangji), a collection of stories and anecdotes from various historical sources.

Proper Nouns

  • 子长 - A name, likely a specific character in the narrative.
  • 武昌 - A place name, historically significant.
  • 寒溪寺 - The name of a temple.
  • 法苑珠林 - A book title, a Buddhist encyclopedia.
  • 广记 - A book title, likely the '太平广记' (Taiping Guangji).

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 骨消肉尽 - Describes a condition where the body has wasted away, with bones and flesh almost entirely gone.
  • 无复方计 - Means 'there is no more hope or plan,' suggesting that the situation is desperate.
  • 夜如得睡眠 - Literally means 'at night, he seemed to fall asleep,' but in the context of a dream, it likely means 'he fell asleep at night.'
  • 梦见数沙门来视其父 - Describes a dream where the person sees several monks visiting his father.
  • 明旦 - Means 'the next morning,' indicating the time after the dream.
  • 便往诣佛图 - Means 'he went to the Buddhist temple,' showing the action taken after the dream.
  • 见诸沙门 - Means 'he saw many monks,' describing the scene at the temple.
  • 问佛为何神 - Means 'he asked what kind of god Buddha is,' indicating the person's curiosity about Buddhism.
  • 沙门为说事状 - Means 'the monks explained the circumstances,' describing the monks' response to the question.
  • 请读经 - Means 'he asked to read the scriptures,' showing the person's request to learn more about Buddhism.
  • 再宿 - Means 'the next day,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 病人自觉病如轻 - Means 'the patient felt his illness was lighter,' describing the improvement in his condition.
  • 昼得小眠 - Means 'he took a short nap during the day,' describing a daytime sleep.
  • 如举头见门中有数十小儿 - Describes a vision where the patient sees many children in the doorway.
  • 皆五彩衣 - Means 'all wearing colorful clothes,' describing the appearance of the children.
  • 手中有持幡仗者 - Means 'some holding flags and weapons,' describing the children's actions.
  • 刀矛者 - Means 'some holding swords and spears,' further describing the children's actions.
  • 于门走入 - Means 'they entered through the door,' describing the movement of the children.
  • 有两小儿在前，径至帘前，忽便还走 - Describes two children walking to the curtain and then suddenly turning back.
  • 语后众人 - Means 'they told the others behind them,' describing the children's communication.
  • 小住小住 - Means 'please wait, please wait,' a polite request.
  • 屋中总是道人 - Means 'there are only monks inside the house,' describing the children's observation.
  • 遂不复来前 - Means 'and they did not come forward again,' indicating the end of the vision.
  • 自此后病渐渐得差 - Means 'from then on, the illness gradually improved,' describing the ongoing recovery.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋南郡 - Refers to a region in the Jin Dynasty, indicating the time and place of the story.
  • 议曹掾 - A specific title in the local administration, indicating the social status of the person involved.
  • 巫医 - Refers to traditional healers and physicians, reflecting the medical practices of the time.
  • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks, indicating the religious context of the story.
  • 佛图 - Refers to a Buddhist temple, indicating the setting of the story.
  • 佛 - Refers to the Buddha, the central figure in Buddhism.
  • 经 - Refers to Buddhist scriptures, indicating the religious texts being read.
  • 珠林九十五 - Refers to a specific chapter or section in a Buddhist text, indicating the source of the story.
  • 广记一百六十一 - Refers to a specific chapter or section in another Buddhist text, indicating another source of the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋南郡 - A region in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 议曹掾 - A title in the local administration.
  • 欧 - The surname of the main character.
  • 佛图 - A Buddhist temple.
  • 沙门 - Buddhist monks.
  • 佛 - The Buddha.
  • 经 - Buddhist scriptures.
  • 珠林九十五 - A specific chapter or section in a Buddhist text.
  • 广记一百六十一 - A specific chapter or section in another Buddhist text.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 驅驢作估於外國 - Driving a donkey to trade in a foreign land.
  • 窈然無底 - Describes a deep, bottomless ravine.
  • 魚貫相連 - Refers to people or things moving in a single file, like fish swimming in a line.
  • 忽有惡鬼牽驢入澗中 - Suddenly an evil spirit led the donkey into the ravine.
  • 胡人性急，便大嗔惡 - Describes the Hua person's quick temper and anger.
  • 尋跡澗中惡鬼 - Searching for the trail of the evil spirit in the ravine.
  • 祝誓呼諸鬼神下逮 - Invoking and calling upon various spirits and deities.
  • 忽然出一平地城門 - Suddenly appeared a gate to a flat land city.
  • 見道人，便乞食 - Saw the Hua person and asked for food.
  • 得食，當與汝 - If you give me food, I will give you something.
  • 鬼王所治 - Refers to the realm or domain of the ghost king.
  • 前見王 - Went to see the king.
  • 驅驢載物，為鬼所奪 - Driving a donkey with goods, which were taken by a ghost.
  • 尋跡至此 - Followed the trail to this place.
  • 少時，疾患，遂薨 - Falling ill and eventually dying.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石虎 - Shi Hu, a ruler of the Later Zhao dynasty, known for his cruelty and military prowess.
  • 胡道人 - Refers to a Hua person, likely a foreigner or a person of a different ethnic background.
  • 惡鬼 - Refers to evil spirits, a common theme in Chinese folklore and religious beliefs.
  • 鬼王 - Refers to the king of ghosts, a figure often found in Chinese mythology and religious texts.
  • 御覽七百三十六 - Refers to the *Yulin* (Imperial Reader), a large-scale encyclopedic work compiled during the Song dynasty.
  • 蔡謨 - Cai Mo, a historical figure who served as a high-ranking official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 廣記三百二十 - Refers to the *Guangji* (Comprehensive Records), another large-scale encyclopedic work compiled during the Song dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 石虎 - Shi Hu, a historical figure.
  • 胡道人 - A Hua person, likely a foreigner or a person of a different ethnic background.
  • 御覽七百三十六 - The *Yulin* (Imperial Reader), an encyclopedic work.
  • 蔡謨 - Cai Mo, a historical figure.
  • 廣記三百二十 - The *Guangji* (Comprehensive Records), an encyclopedic work.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 舉家 - Means the entire family.
  • 唯有 - Emphasizes exclusivity.
  • 渐瘦 - Means gradually became thin.
  • 长丈余而有四面 - Means tall over a zhang (about 3 meters) and with four faces.
  • 面皆有七孔 - Means each face had seven holes.
  • 自号 - Indicates self-proclaimed titles.
  • 来辄见吞 - Means when he came, he was immediately swallowed.
  • 迳出下部 - Means he came out through the lower part.
  • 慎勿道我 - Means be careful not to tell anyone about me.
  • 当长留腹中 - Means I will stay in your stomach forever.
  • 阖门骇惋 - Means the whole family was shocked and grieved.
  • 遂移避 - Means they then moved away.
  • 淩晨 - Means early morning.
  • 自出看 - Means he went out to see.
  • 乌帻 - Refers to black headgear.
  • 渚 - Means a small island or shore.
  • 官使 - Means official envoys.
  • 捉枻 - Means to hold the oar.
  • 緪挽 - Means to pull or drag.
  • 暂住逆旅 - Means they temporarily stayed at an inn.
  • 平望亭 - A place name, indicating a specific location.
  • 潜逃得归 - Means they secretly escaped and returned home.
  • 十余日外 - Means after more than ten days.
  • 复有呼声 - Means there were again calls.
  • 汝何敢委叛 - Means how dare you abandon and rebel.
  • 将至船 - Means they brought him to the boat.
  • 犹多菽 - Means there were still many beans.
  • 慎勿复走 - Means be careful not to run away again.
  • 若有饮食，当相唤 - Means if there is food or drink, we will call you.
  • 须臾 - Means in a moment.
  • 一吏呼剿上 - Means one official called Jiao up.
  • 高门瓦屋 - Refers to a high gate and tiled house.
  • 欢宴盈堂 - Means a joyful banquet filled the hall.
  • 仍令剿行酒 - Means they made Jiao serve the wine.
  • 并赐炙啖 - Means and gave him roasted meat to eat.
  • 天将晓 - Means when dawn was approaching.
  • 汝且停 - Means you stay here for now.
  • 顷之 - Means in a short while.
  • 但见高坟森木 - Means he only saw high graves and dense trees.
  • 剿心迷乱 - Means Jiao's heart was confused.
  • 经日方得 - Means after many days they found him.
  • 寻发大疮而死 - Means he soon developed a large wound and died.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河内 - Refers to Hedong, an ancient region in northern China.
  • 姚元起 - The name of a person, Yao Yuanqi, who lived in Hedong.
  • 吴兴武唐闾剿 - The name of a person, Wu Xing Wu Tang Lu Jiao, who lived in Wuxing.
  • 高天大将军 - A self-proclaimed title, indicating a supernatural or mythical figure.
  • 逆旅 - Refers to an inn or temporary lodging, a common term in classical Chinese literature.
  • 平望亭 - A place name, indicating a specific location.
  • 嘉乐故家 - A place name, indicating an old house.
  • 高门瓦屋 - Refers to a high gate and tiled house.
  • 欢宴 - Refers to a joyful banquet.
  • 炙啖 - Refers to roasted meat.
  • 高坟森木 - Refers to high graves and dense trees.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南平国蛮兵 - Refers to the barbarian soldiers from the Nanping Kingdom.
  • 义熙初 - Refers to the early years of the Yihxi era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 鬼附之 - Means 'a ghost attached to them,' indicating a supernatural event.
  • 声呦呦细长 - Describes a sound that is soft and long, similar to the cry of a deer.
  • 若占吉凶，辄先索琵琶，随弹而言 - This phrase describes a ritual where the ghost requests a lute (琵琶) before making predictions, suggesting a specific cultural practice.
  • 不久持节也 - Means 'you will soon hold a banner,' a metaphor for a high position or authority.
  • 予为国郎中，亲领此土 - Means 'I was a Langzhong of the state, personally overseeing this land,' indicating a personal role in the administration.
  • 荆州俗语云 - Refers to a local saying or belief from the Jingzhou region.
  • 是老鼠所作，名曰鬼侯 - Means 'it is made by a mouse, called Ghost Lord,' a local belief about the origin of the ghost.
  • 广记三百二十二 - Refers to a specific entry in the 'Guangji' (Widely Recorded), a collection of anecdotes and stories.
  • 平原陈皋 - Refers to a person named Chen Gao from the Pingyuan region.
  • 义熙中 - Refers to the middle years of the Yihxi era.
  • 从广陵樊梁后乘船出 - Means 'he traveled by boat from Guangling after following the riverbank.'
  • 忽有一赤鬼 - Means 'suddenly there appeared a red ghost.'
  • 长可丈许 - Means 'about a zhang long,' where a zhang is an ancient Chinese unit of length.
  • 首戴绛冠，形如鹿角 - Describes the ghost wearing a red crown and having antlers like a deer.
  • 就皋求载 - Means 'it asked Gao to carry it.'
  • 倏尔上船 - Means 'it suddenly boarded the boat.'
  • 素能禁气 - Means 'he was skilled in controlling his breath,' referring to a spiritual or martial practice.
  • 因歌俗家南地之曲 - Means 'he sang a folk song from the southern region,' indicating regional cultural differences.
  • 鬼乃吐舌张眼，以杖竿掷之，即四散，成火照于野 - Describes the ghost's reaction to the song, which involves it opening its mouth and eyes, throwing a staff, and causing a fire.
  • 皋无几而死 - Means 'Gao died shortly after.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 义熙 - A regnal title used by Emperor Xiaowu of Liu Song during the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 南平国 - Refers to a kingdom in southern China, possibly a reference to a local state or region.
  • 南蛮校尉 - A military title in the Chinese bureaucracy, responsible for managing the southern barbarians.
  • 荆州 - A historical region in central China, known for its strategic importance.
  • 广陵 - An ancient city in Jiangsu Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 樊梁 - A river in the area, often mentioned in historical texts.
  • 禁气 - A practice involving controlling one's breath, often associated with martial arts or spiritual practices.
  • 俗家南地之曲 - Refers to a folk song from the southern region, indicating regional cultural differences.

Proper Nouns

  • 南平国 - The name of a kingdom or region.
  • 义熙 - A regnal title.
  • 姑熟 - A place name, possibly a city or town.
  • 南蛮校尉 - A military title.
  • 荆州 - A historical region.
  • 广陵 - A historical city.
  • 樊梁 - A river.
  • 平原陈皋 - The name of a person.
  • 广记三百二十二 - A specific entry in a historical text.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太元十二年 - Specific year in the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Eastern Jin (377 AD).
  • 有道人外国 - Literally 'there is a foreign monk,' referring to a person from a foreign land who practices Buddhism.
  • 能吞刀吐火，吐珠玉金银 - Describes supernatural abilities, including swallowing and spitting out knives and fire, and spitting out pearls, jade, and silver.
  • 自说其所受术 - Means 'he spoke of the techniques he had learned.'
  • 御览引作师即白衣，非沙门也 - Note indicates that the *Taiping Guangji* mentions that the person is a layperson (白衣) and not a monk (沙门).
  • 尝行，见一人担担 - Means 'once while traveling, he saw a man carrying a load.'
  • 可受升余 - Means 'can hold a little more than a sheng (a unit of volume).'
  • 吾步行疲极 - Means 'I am extremely tired from walking.'
  • 欲寄君担 - Means 'I want to entrust my load to you.'
  • 君若见许 - Means 'if you will allow me.'
  • 笼不更大，其人亦不更小 - Means 'the cage is not bigger, and the person is not smaller.'
  • 担之亦不觉重于先 - Means 'the load does not feel heavier than before.'
  • 既行数十里 - Means 'after traveling for several miles.'
  • 树下住食 - Means 'they stopped to eat under a tree.'
  • 我自有食 - Means 'I have my own food.'
  • 不肯出 - Means 'he did not come out.'
  • 止住笼中 - Means 'he stayed in the cage.'
  • 饮食器物罗列 - Means 'food and utensils were arranged.'
  • 肴膳丰腆亦办 - Means 'the dishes were plentiful and well-prepared.'
  • 反呼担人食 - Means 'he then called the man to eat.'
  • 未半 - Means 'not halfway through.'
  • 我欲与妇共食 - Means 'I want to eat with my wife.'
  • 即复口吐出一女子 - Means 'he then spat out a woman.'
  • 年二十许 - Means 'about twenty years old.'
  • 衣裳容貌甚美 - Means 'dressed beautifully and very attractive.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太元 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of Eastern Jin (372-396 AD).
  • 道人 - Refers to a Buddhist monk or practitioner.
  • 吞刀吐火，吐珠玉金银 - These are supernatural abilities often associated with Buddhist monks in Chinese folklore.
  • 御览 - The *Taiping Guangji* (太平广记), a large collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 白衣 - Refers to a layperson, as opposed to a monk (沙门).

Proper Nouns

  • 太元 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of Eastern Jin.
  • 御览 - The *Taiping Guangji*.
  • 白衣 - Layperson.
  • 沙门 - Monk.

[12]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 婦便口中出一年少丈夫 - Fantastical description of a woman giving birth to a young man from her mouth.
  • 共食笼中 - Metaphorical way of describing coexistence in a cage.
  • 便有三人宽急之事，亦复不异 - Suggests three people (the woman, the young man, and the husband) have similar experiences or situations.
  • 有顷，其夫动，如欲觉 - Indicates the husband begins to move, as if he is about to wake up.
  • 妇便以外夫内口中 - Describes the woman putting the husband back into her mouth.
  • 夫起，语担人曰：“可去。” - Indicates the husband gets up and tells the person carrying the load that they can leave.
  • 即以妇内口中，次及食器物 - Describes the husband putting the woman back into his mouth and then the food and utensils.
  • 此人既至国中，有一家大富贵，财巨万 - Introduces a wealthy family in the country, emphasizing their great wealth.
  • 而性悭○ - Describes the family as stingy or miserly.
  • 不行仁义 - Indicates the family does not practice benevolence or righteousness.
  • 语担人云：“吾试为君破奴悭囊。” - Suggests the person carrying the load offers to break the family's stinginess.
  • 即至其家 - Describes the person carrying the load going to the family's home.
  • 有一好马，甚珍之，系在柱下 - Describes a valuable horse that is tied to a pillar.
  • 忽失去，寻索不知处 - Indicates the horse suddenly disappears and cannot be found.
  • 明日，见马在五斗罂中，终不可破取 - Describes the horse being found in a large jar, which cannot be broken to retrieve it.
  • 不知何方得取之 - Indicates it is unclear how to retrieve the horse.
  • 君作百人厨，以周一方穷乏马当得出耳 - Suggests the person carrying the load offers to prepare a meal for a hundred people to help the family and retrieve the horse.
  • 主人即狼狈作之，毕，马还在柱下 - Describes the family hurriedly preparing the meal, and after it is done, the horse is found back at the pillar.
  • 明旦，其父母老在堂上 - Describes the next morning, the parents are found in the hall.
  • 忽复不见；举家惶怖，不知所在 - Describes the parents suddenly disappearing, causing the family to be terrified and not know where they are.
  • 开妆器，忽然见父母在泽壶中，不知何由得出 - Describes the parents being found in a makeup box, and it is unclear how to get them out.
  • 复往请之，其人云：“君当更作千人饮食，以饴百姓穷者，乃当得出。” - Suggests the person carrying the load offers to prepare a meal for a thousand people to help the family and retrieve the parents.
  • 既作，其父母自在床上也 - Describes the family preparing the meal, and after it is done, the parents are found on the bed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妇便口中出一年少丈夫 - Fantastical element rooted in folklore or myth, where women can give birth to children in unusual ways.
  • 共食笼中 - Metaphorical description of coexistence, possibly reflecting ancient beliefs about the relationship between humans and animals.
  • 此人既至国中，有一家大富贵，财巨万 - Describes a wealthy family, a common theme in classical Chinese literature, often used to illustrate the contrast between wealth and virtue.
  • 而性悭○ - Describes the family as stingy, a common character trait in classical Chinese stories, often used to highlight the importance of generosity and charity.
  • 不行仁义 - Indicates the family does not practice benevolence or righteousness, a common moral theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 语担人云：“吾试为君破奴悭囊。” - Suggests the person carrying the load offers to break the family's stinginess, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.

  • 有一好马，甚珍之，系在柱下 - Describes a valuable horse, a common symbol of wealth and status in classical Chinese literature.
  • 忽失去，寻索不知处 - Describes the horse suddenly disappearing, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.

  • 不知何方得取之 - Indicates it is unclear how to retrieve the horse, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.
  • 君作百人厨，以周一方穷乏马当得出耳 - Suggests the person carrying the load offers to prepare a meal for a hundred people to help the family and retrieve the horse, a common moral lesson in classical Chinese literature.
  • 主人即狼狈作之，毕，马还在柱下 - Describes the family hurriedly preparing the meal, and after it is done, the horse is found back at the pillar, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.
  • 明旦，其父母老在堂上 - Describes the next morning, the parents are found in the hall, a common narrative element in classical Chinese literature.
  • 忽复不见；举家惶怖，不知所在 - Describes the parents suddenly disappearing, causing the family to be terrified and not know where they are, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.
  • 开妆器，忽然见父母在泽壶中，不知何由得出 - Describes the parents being found in a makeup box, and it is unclear how to get them out, a fantastical element that may be rooted in folklore or myth.
  • 复往请之，其人云：“君当更作千人饮食，以饴百姓穷者，乃当得出。” - Suggests the person carrying the load offers to prepare a meal for a thousand people to help the family and retrieve the parents, a common moral lesson in classical Chinese literature.
  • 既作，其父母自在床上也 - Describes the family preparing the meal, and after it is done, the parents are found on the bed, a common plot device in classical Chinese stories.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 妇便口中出一年少丈夫，共食笼中 - Fantastical description of a woman giving birth to a young man from her mouth and they ate together in a cage.
  • 便有三人宽急之事，亦复不异 - There were three people with similar experiences or situations.
  • 有顷，其夫动，如欲觉 - After a while, the husband began to move, as if he was about to wake up.
  • 妇便以外夫内口中 - The woman put the husband back into her mouth.
  • 夫起，语担人曰：“可去。” - The husband got up and told the person carrying the load that they could leave.
  • 即以妇内口中，次及食器物 - He put the woman back into his mouth and then the food and utensils.
  • 此人既至国中，有一家大富贵，财巨万 - This person arrived in the country and found a family that was very wealthy, with great wealth.
  • 而性悭○ - But they were stingy.
  • 不行仁义 - They did not practice benevolence or righteousness.
  • 语担人云：“吾试为君破奴悭囊。” - He told the person carrying the load, 'I will try to break your family's stinginess.'
  • 即至其家 - He went to the family's home.
  • 有一好马，甚珍之，系在柱下 - There was a valuable horse, which they cherished, tied to a pillar.

[13]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 戒行清苦 - Refers to a monk who practices strict discipline and leads a life of austerity.
  • 事蛊 - Means to practice or engage in witchcraft, specifically the use of poisonous substances.
  • 人啖其食饮，无不吐血而死 - Means that anyone who consumes the food or drink will inevitably die by vomiting blood.
  • 诣之 - Means to visit or go to someone's place.
  • 咒焉 - Refers to the act of reciting a spell or incantation.
  • 一双蜈蚣 - Means 'a pair of centipedes.'
  • 长尺余 - Refers to a length of more than one foot.
  • 于盘中走出 - Means the centipedes emerged from the plate.
  • 绝食而归 - Means to stop eating and return home.
  • 竟无他 - Means that nothing else happened, implying the person remained unharmed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 沙门昙游 - Refers to a Buddhist monk named Tan You.
  • 剡县 - Refers to Yandian County, a place in ancient China.
  • 蛊 - Refers to a form of witchcraft involving the use of poisonous substances, often associated with dark magic.
  • 蜈蚣 - Refers to centipedes, which are often associated with danger and evil in Chinese culture.
  • 盘 - Refers to a plate or dish.

Proper Nouns

  • 沙门昙游 - The name of the Buddhist monk.
  • 剡县 - The name of the county where the story takes place.
  • 蛊 - The term for the witchcraft practiced by the family.

[14]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 累世为蛊以致富 - Indicates that the family has been engaged in the practice of raising蛊 (a type of magical or poisonous creature) for generations, which has brought them wealth.
  • 子女丰悦 - Describes the children and grandchildren as being well-off and happy.
  • 不以此语之 - Indicates that the new wife was not informed about the family's practice of raising蛊.
  • 悉行，妇独守家 - Describes the situation where everyone left, leaving the new wife alone at home.
  • 见屋中一大堽 - Refers to seeing a large mound or heap in the house.
  • 试发，见一大蛇 - Describes the action of opening the mound and discovering a large snake inside.
  • 作沸汤，悉灌杀之 - Describes the act of boiling water and pouring it over the snake to kill it.
  • 无几，其家疾病亡略尽 - Indicates that shortly after, the entire family began to suffer from illness and most died.
  • 人姓邹坐齐中 - Describes a person named邹 (Zou) who was sitting in the middle of a place called齐 (Qi).
  • 忽有一人通刺诣之 - Describes a sudden visit by someone who presented a card to邹.
  • 题刺云舒甄仲 - The name on the card was舒甄仲 (Shu Zhenzhong).
  • 既去，疑其非人 - After the visitor left,邹 suspected that the visitor was not human.
  • 寻其刺 - Describes the act of searching for the card.
  • 吾知之矣，是予舍西土瓦中人 - Indicates that邹 realized the visitor was a person from the west of his house, hidden in a clay pot.
  • 便往令人将锸掘之 - Describes邹 sending someone with a spade to dig up the clay pot.
  • 果于瓦器中得桐人，长尺余 - Describes finding a wooden figure about a foot long inside the clay pot.
  • 郗世了在会稽造墓 - Describes a person named郗世了 (Xi Shiliao) who was building a tomb in the region of会稽 (Kuiji).
  • 其地多石 - Indicates that the area where the tomb was being built had many stones.
  • 后破大石，得一龟 - Describes breaking a large stone and finding a turtle inside.
  • 长尺二寸许 - Describes the turtle as being about one foot and two inches long.
  • 在石中，石了无孔也 - Indicates that the turtle was inside the stone, and the stone had no holes.
  • 得非龟石俱生乎 - A rhetorical question suggesting that perhaps the turtle and the stone were both naturally formed together.
  • 既破出之，龟行动与常龟无异 - Describes the turtle moving normally after being freed from the stone.
  • 石受龟如人刻安之 - Indicates that the stone seemed to have been carved and placed there by someone.
  • 濡须口有一大舶船覆在水中 - Describes a large ship that had capsized in the water at濡须口 (Ruxukou).
  • 水小时便出见 - Indicates that the ship would appear when the water level was low.
  • 尝有渔人夜宿其傍 - Describes a fisherman who once stayed overnight near the ship.
  • 以船系之 - Describes the fisherman tying his boat to the capsized ship.
  • 但闻筝笛弦管之音 - Describes hearing the sounds of zithers, flutes, and other musical instruments.
  • 梦人驱遣云：“勿近官妓！” - Describes a dream where someone told the fisherman not to get close to the official entertainers.
  • 此人惊觉，即移船去 - Describes the fisherman waking up in a fright and immediately moving his boat away.
  • 传云是曹公载妓船覆于此，于今存在 - Indicates that the ship was said to be a boat that 曹公 (Cao Cao) had used to transport entertainers, which had capsized there and still existed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蛊 - Refers to a type of magical or poisonous creature raised for various purposes, including wealth and power. The practice of raising蛊 is often associated with dark magic and is considered taboo.
  • 齐 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific location or region.
  • 舒甄仲 - A name that may refer to a specific individual or a character in a story.
  • 会稽 - A historical region in China, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 曹公 - A title often used to refer to 曹操 (Cao Cao), a prominent historical figure during the Three Kingdoms period.

Proper Nouns

  • 荥阳郡 - A historical region in China.
  • 廖 - A family name.
  • 邹 - A personal name.
  • 齐 - A place name.
  • 舒甄仲 - A personal name.
  • 郗世了 - A personal name.
  • 会稽 - A historical region in China.

[15]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有一客往吊之 - Standard expression meaning 'there was a guest who came to mourn him.'
  • 忽通从合中出，以纶巾系头 - Describes Li Tong suddenly appearing from a room, wearing a silk scarf.
  • 书钞一百二十九引虚异志 - Refers to a specific reference in the 'Shu Chao' (Book of Quotes) Volume 129, which cites the 'Xu Yi Zhi' (Supplementary Strange Records).
  • 案陈氏本书钞及俞氏唐类函并作述异志盖以意改疑亦是灵鬼志也 - Explains that Chen's 'Book of Quotes' and Yu's 'Tang Lei Han' both refer to the 'Shu Yi Zhi' (Narrative of Strange Events), which is likely the same as 'Ling Gui Zhi' (Spiritual Ghosts Record).
  • 历阳县张应，先是魔家，取佛家女为妇 - Describes Zhang Ying, who was originally from the demon family, marrying a Buddhist woman.
  • 咸和八年，移居芜湖 - Indicates the year 333 AD when Zhang Ying moved to Wuhu.
  • 妻病，因为魔事，家财略尽不差 - Describes the wife's illness and the subsequent magical rituals that depleted their wealth.
  • 我本佛家女，乞为我作佛事 - The wife's request to perform Buddhist rituals.
  • 应便往精舍中见竺法镜 - Zhang Ying went to a temple to meet Master Zhi Fajing.
  • 佛普济众生，问君当一心受持身戒耳 - A Buddhist teaching emphasizing the importance of self-discipline and moral conduct.
  • 昙镜期明当向其家 - Master Zhi Fajing promised to visit the next day.
  • 应先手巧眠觉，便把火作高座，及鬼子母座 - Zhang Ying, being skilled, woke up and made high seats and a seat for the goddess of children.
  • 闻应说梦，遂夫妻受五戒 - After hearing about the dream, the couple took the Five Precepts.
  • 病亦寻差 - The wife's illness soon improved.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李通 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official.
  • 魔家 - Refers to a family associated with demonic or supernatural practices.
  • 佛家 - Refers to a family associated with Buddhism.
  • 咸和八年 - The year 333 AD during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 芜湖 - A city in Anhui Province, China.
  • 魔事 - Magical or ritualistic practices.
  • 精舍 - A temple or monastery.
  • 竺法镜 - A Buddhist monk.
  • 佛普济众生 - A Buddhist teaching about the universal salvation of all beings.
  • 五戒 - The Five Precepts in Buddhism, which are basic moral guidelines.
  • 鬼子母 - A Buddhist deity known as the Mother of Ghosts, often invoked for protection and fertility.

  • 李通 - A historical figure.
  • 魔家 - A family associated with demonic or supernatural practices.
  • 佛家 - A family associated with Buddhism.
  • 咸和八年 - The year 333 AD.
  • 芜湖 - A city in Anhui Province, China.
  • 魔事 - Magical or ritualistic practices.
  • 精舍 - A temple or monastery.
  • 竺法镜 - A Buddhist monk.
  • 佛普济众生 - A Buddhist teaching.
  • 五戒 - The Five Precepts in Buddhism.
  • 鬼子母 - A Buddhist deity.
